checkGuestList.name: Check the Guest List
checkGuestList.description: Write a function that checks if a person's name is on the guest list. Return true if they are, false if they aren't.

nameSingleListTrue.name: Person is on a single person list
nameSingleListTrue.description: Return true if a person is on the guest list.

nameSingleListFalse.name: Person is not on a single person list
nameSingleListFalse.description: Return false if a person is not on the guest list.

nameListTrue.name: Person is on a larger list
nameListTrue.description: Return true if a person is on a larger guest list.

nameListFalse.name: Person is not on a larger list
nameListFalse.description: Return false if a person is not on a larger guest list.

realLifeList.question: How would I do this in real life with a paper list?
realLifeList.answer: You'd read down the list, one name at a time, comparing each to the name you're looking for. As soon as you found a match, you'd stop. If you reached the end without a match, the person isn't on the list. Your code does the same thing.

loopAndCompare.question: How do I loop through the list and compare each entry?
loopAndCompare.answer: A for-each loop gives you each name in turn. Inside, use an if-statement to compare the current name to the target.

returnTrueVsFalse.question: When should I return true vs false?
returnTrueVsFalse.answer: Return `true` as soon as you find a match. There's no reason to keep looking. Only return `false` after the loop has finished and you've checked every name without finding it. (A common mistake is returning `false` inside the loop, which gives up after just the first name.)
